hero.headline: Transform Your Garage in 1-2 Days
hero.subheadline: Polyurea + polyaspartic systems built to last. Backed by 94 five-star reviews, a premium workmanship warranty, and 30 years of Matt Gifford doing this work himself.

problem.headline: Why most garage floors look like hell 3 years in
points: Cheap DIY epoxy kits peel within 18-36 months. They're UV-sensitive and fail from hot tire lift.
85% of coating failures come from bad prep — and DIY kits skip the diamond grinding and moisture testing that actually matter.
Oil stains, tire marks, and concrete dust eat away at untreated concrete every day you wait.
Once a bad coating fails, removing it costs more than the original install did.

solution.headline: The MGP polyaspartic system. Why it's different.
points: Full diamond-grind prep. We strip your floor to bare concrete and moisture-test it — most competitors skip this because it's expensive.
Two-layer polyurea + polyaspartic system. Base: industrial polyurea for toughness and adhesion. Top: polyaspartic for UV stability and hot tire resistance.
Premium workmanship warranty. If it peels, chips, or fails within the warranty period — we come back and fix it. Free. In writing.
Done in 1-2 days. Drop your cars at 7am. Pick up a showroom-quality garage at 5pm the next day.
Chip colors, gloss levels, and custom finishes. Your garage, your taste.

guarantees.title: Premium Workmanship Warranty
guarantees.body: If it peels, cracks, fades, or chips within the warranty period — we redo it. Free. No fine print.

faqs.q: How much does a garage floor coating cost?
faqs.a: Most residential 2-car garages run $3,200-$5,800 depending on square footage, concrete condition, and chip choices. Detailed quotes are always free and Matt does them in person.

faqs.q: Is polyaspartic really better than epoxy?
faqs.a: For garages exposed to heat, UV, and hot tires — yes, by a lot. Polyaspartic is UV-stable where epoxy alone yellows and cracks. That's why our system pairs an industrial polyurea basecoat for toughness with a polyaspartic topcoat for UV durability.

faqs.q: How long does the floor last?
faqs.a: Our full system is backed by a premium workmanship warranty. Real-world data from our installs: 8-year-old Central Coast garages still look like day one.
Most residential garages are done in 1-2 days. Walk on it after 24 hours, drive on it after 3 days, fully cured in 5-7 days.

faqs.q: Do I need to prep anything?
faqs.a: Just move everything out of the garage the day before. We handle grinding, repair, cleanup, everything.
Material AND workmanship. If it peels, chips, delaminates, or fades unnaturally within the warranty period, we come back and make it right. Written, not a handshake.

bonuses.name: Custom Project Quote With No Pressure

offer.scarcity: Matt personally installs every pool deck. Only 2 pool deck slots per week during peak season.

problem.headline: Why most pool decks become painful — literally
points: Untreated concrete bakes in the Central Coast sun and can scorch bare feet at 140°F+ on a 90° day.
Smooth concrete + pool water = fall hazard, especially for kids running between the pool and the patio.
Pool chemicals (chlorine, muriatic acid) slowly eat untreated concrete. The cracks you're ignoring now turn into expensive repairs in 2-3 years.
Cheap pool-deck paint peels in a single summer. DIY kits aren't built for constant water, sun, and chemical exposure.

solution.headline: Our pool deck system. Cool, grippy, chlorine-proof.
points: Cool-touch textured coating. Reflects heat and adds grip — meaningfully safer for bare feet and kids.
Chlorine + UV stable. Formulated for decades of pool chemistry and direct California sun.
Crack repair included. We fix what's broken before we coat — no hiding problems under a pretty finish.
Custom texture + color. Match your home, your landscaping, or go resort-style bold.
Premium workmanship warranty on material and installation. If it fails within the warranty period, we fix it.

guarantees.body: Material and workmanship. Peeling, cracking, fading — we come back and make it right.

leadMagnet.subtitle: The questions a real pro will welcome — and a part-timer will dodge. Use these on every pool deck quote.
bullets: The chemistry question (epoxy or polyurea?) that separates 5-year coatings from 15-year coatings
How long the install actually takes — and the timeline that signals 'no real prep'
The CSLB license question, plus how to verify it yourself in 60 seconds
What a real installer-backed warranty covers — and what 'manufacturer warranty' really means
How many local Google reviews are enough — and how to spot the fakes

faqs.q: How much does pool deck coating cost?
faqs.a: Most SLO County pool decks run $4,000-$8,500 depending on square footage, condition of the existing concrete, and texture choices. Every quote is free and on-site.

faqs.q: How much cooler will it actually be?
faqs.a: Cool-touch coatings typically reduce surface temp meaningfully versus bare concrete. In Central Coast summer sun, that's the difference between walking barefoot and hopping for the nearest towel.

faqs.q: Is it really slip-resistant when wet?
faqs.a: Any coated surface can be slippery when wet. Our textured topcoats provide significantly better grip than bare or painted concrete, but no coating is 100% slip-proof. We'll show you actual samples during your consultation.

faqs.q: Will pool chemistry hurt the coating?
faqs.a: Not ours. We use chlorine-stable, UV-stable systems formulated specifically for pool environments. That's why our workmanship warranty stands behind it.

faqs.q: How long until we can use the pool again?
faqs.a: Most decks are done in 1-2 days. Walk on it after 24 hours. Back to normal pool use in 5-7 days once fully cured.

faqs.q: Can you work around our existing decking or landscaping?
faqs.a: Yes. We protect everything adjacent — tile coping, plants, pool equipment — and clean up like we were never there.

problem.headline: Your driveway is the first thing buyers and guests see
points: Oil stains, tire marks, and concrete dust send a signal: this house is tired.
Untreated concrete cracks faster under Central Coast heat cycles and winter rain — once cracks spread, repair cost climbs fast.
Hot tires peel most DIY coatings within a year. Cheap driveway paint = money in the trash.
The longer you wait, the more the concrete degrades underneath — and prep cost goes up.

solution.headline: Our driveway system. Built for hot tires, oil, and UV.
points: Hot tire resistant polyaspartic topcoat — the only coating system proven to survive hot tire lift.
Oil-proof + chemical-resistant. Spills wipe clean like tile.
UV stable. Won't yellow, chalk, or fade in Central Coast sun like cheap acrylic sealers do.
Diamond-grind prep + crack repair included. We fix what's broken, then coat.
Custom color, gloss, and chip options to complement your home's exterior.

bullets: The chemistry question (epoxy or polyurea?) that separates 5-year coatings from 15-year coatings
How long the install actually takes — and the timeline that signals 'no real prep'

faqs.q: How much does a driveway coating cost?
faqs.a: Most SLO County driveways run $3,800-$9,500 depending on square footage and concrete condition. Every quote is free and done on-site so you get an exact number, not an estimate.

faqs.q: Will it really survive hot tires?
faqs.a: Yes — if you use the right system. Cheap epoxy fails from hot tire lift almost immediately. Our polyurea base + polyaspartic topcoat is specifically rated for hot tire contact, which is why our workmanship warranty stands behind it.
Oil sits on top of our coating — it doesn't soak in. Spills wipe clean with any household degreaser. Same for brake fluid, transmission fluid, coolant.

faqs.q: Can you coat a cracked driveway?
faqs.a: Yes. We repair cracks with structural epoxy before coating. If the concrete is severely damaged, we'll tell you honestly whether coating is the right call or whether you need replacement first.
Most driveways are done in 1-2 days. Walk on it after 24 hours, drive on it after 3 days.

faqs.q: Does it add to resale value?
faqs.a: Curb appeal is one of the highest-ROI upgrades for a home. A professionally coated driveway is often the first detail buyers mention positively.
